seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: Ensure the email avoids technical jargon and is easy to understand.
new_constraint: The email must include at least one technical term related to the project.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. Ensure the email avoids technical jargon and is easy to understand. The email must include at least one technical term related to the project.
response:
Subject: Request for Extension - [Your Name] - [Project Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully ask if it would be possible to get a short extension on the [Project Name] project, which is currently due in two days.

I have been working hard on the project, but I've run into a bit more difficulty than I anticipated with the [mention a specific part of the project, e.g., data analysis, literature review]. Specifically, I'm having trouble getting the **algorithm** to run smoothly, and I want to make sure I submit my best possible work.

Would it be possible to have an extension until [Suggest a specific new date, e.g., the end of the week, or Monday]? This extra time would allow me to properly refine the [mention the specific part again] and ensure the final submission meets your expectations.

I apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause. Thank you very much for considering my situation.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>

evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The Model Response includes a technical term ("algorithm") as required by the instruction, but it does not avoid technical jargon as instructed. The constraints of avoiding technical jargon while including a technical term are conflicting, but the model does not acknowledge this conflict. Instead, it directly provides a response to the instruction without addressing or resolving the conflicting constraints.

**Answer**: 1
